steps.title: Apply climate-driven range shifts
steps.content: Soto and Halpern argue that climate change can move species into new ranges and make new habitats suitable. That makes fixed invasive/native labels less stable than they may seem.

steps.title: Keep the conclusion broad
steps.content: A invents a past Colorado population of rusty crayfish. C reinforces protection from invasive species rather than questioning classifications. D limits the issue to one species without support; the researchers' point concerns the classification framework more generally. The answer is B.
